name: Cuntis - San Xurxo de Codeseda Church Loop
name_original: Cuntis - vuelta de la Iglesia de San Xurxo de Codeseda
description: Cycle 25.6 miles (41.2 km) through Galicia's hilly landscapes to the historic Church of San Xurxo de Codeseda, a moderate road ride.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cuntis traverse a varied landscape in Galicia, Spain, characterized by its position in the Ulla-Umia region. The terrain includes gentle slopes and more demanding ascents, offering diverse cycling experiences. River valleys, such as those of the Gallo and Umia rivers, define many sections of the routes. The area features a mix of open countryside and forested areas, providing a range of scenic backdrops for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Cuntis The most popular road cycling route is Cuntis - San Xurxo de Codeseda Church Loop, a 25.6 miles (41.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 59 minutes to complete. This route navigates through the region's hilly landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pico Sacro and Ulla River Loop, a difficult 40.8 miles (65.6 km) path. This route features challenging ascents towards Pico Sacro and views along the Ulla River. Local road cyclists also love the Caldas de Reis - Cuntis - return via the Umia River, a 17.7 miles (28.5 km) trail leading through river valleys and the Ulla-Umia region, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Road cycling around Cuntis is defined by hilly landscapes, river valleys, and areas surrounding thermal springs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents. The routes in Cuntis are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 23 reviews. More than 1,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cuntis's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Cuntis?
A: Cuntis offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 140 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult rides.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Cuntis?
A: The road cycling routes in Cuntis present a diverse range of challenges. You'll find 11 easy routes, 67 moderate routes, and 64 difficult routes, ensuring options for every fitness level. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974485276/pico-sacro-and-ulla-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Sacro and Ulla River Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging option with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Cuntis?
A: Yes, Cuntis has 11 easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a more relaxed ride. These routes often feature gentler slopes and less demanding terrain, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without excessive effort.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling around Cuntis?
A: Road cycling around Cuntis takes you through a varied landscape in Galicia, characterized by hilly terrain, river valleys like the Gallo and Umia, and areas surrounding natural thermal springs. You'll experience a mix of open countryside and forested areas, providing diverse scenic backdrops.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Cuntis?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Cuntis are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974512598/cuntis-san-xurxo-de-codeseda-church-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuntis - San Xurxo de Codeseda Church Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which navigates through the region's hilly landscapes.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks along the road cycling routes in Cuntis?
A: Cuntis and its surroundings offer several notable landmarks and scenic spots. You can encounter historical churches, such as Santa Mariña de Carracedo, and the Monument to the Sacred Heart of Jesus, which provides panoramic views. For natural beauty, consider routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2030121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barosa Waterfall and Mills&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4040627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Burgas Hot Spring Fountain&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cuntis?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling in Cuntis, offering pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather. While summer can be warm, the varied terrain and proximity to coastal areas can still provide enjoyable conditions. Winter cycling is possible, but conditions may vary.

Q: Is Cuntis suitable for family-friendly road cycling?
A: While Cuntis offers routes for all levels, including easy options, families should consider the specific route's elevation changes and traffic. The region's diverse terrain means some routes are more challenging. Look for the 11 easy-rated routes for the most family-friendly experiences.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Cuntis?
A: Parking is generally available in Cuntis town center and at various trailheads or points of interest throughout the region. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974527170/caldas-de-reis-cuntis-return-via-the-umia-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caldas de Reis - Cuntis - return via the Umia River&lt;/a&gt;, often start from accessible locations where parking can be found.

Q: What unique attractions can I explore near the cycling routes in Cuntis?
A: Beyond cycling, Cuntis is famous for its natural thermal waters, with the Termas de Cuntis complex offering balneotherapy. You can also visit historical sites like the Church and Cemetery of Adina with Roman and Suebian archaeological remains, or natural wonders such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2030121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barosa Waterfall and Mills&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Cuntis?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Cuntis, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 23 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from gentle slopes to challenging ascents, and the scenic beauty of the river valleys and thermal spring areas. The diverse options for different ability levels are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there cafes, pubs, or accommodation options for cyclists in Cuntis?
A: Cuntis, being a town renowned for its thermal springs, offers various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options like hotels and guesthouses. These establishments are generally cyclist-friendly, providing places to refuel and rest after your rides.
tips: Straddling the interior of Galicia and the coast, Cuntis enjoys a very good location in a strategic position between Santiago de Compostela (27 km) and Pontevedra (30 km). Famous for its natural and medicinal hot springs
9th century church. Good stop to fill bottles with water from the church fountain

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies the "Cuntis - Tour of the Church of San Xurxo de Codeseda" as a moderate road cycling route. This means it requires good fitness and may include challenging segments with significant elevation changes, including gradients of 15-20% in some parts.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is characteristic of Galicia, featuring a 'constant rollercoaster of steep hills and mountains' and 'relentless hills'. While primarily a road cycling route, be prepared for varied road conditions. Some sections might be unpaved or have poor asphalt quality, so a sturdy bike is recommended.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a scenic journey through Galicia's diverse landscapes. Key highlights include the historic Cuntis Historic Center and Thermal Fountains. The primary destination is the Church of San Jorge de Codeseda and Fountain, a Romanesque church dating back to 1175 with a neoclassical façade. Nearby, you'll also find the ancient 'Carballeira da Feira' oak grove.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the exact trailhead are not extensively documented, Cuntis town center is a logical starting point. You should be able to find parking options within the town to begin your tour.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The rural setting generally offers peaceful roads with low traffic, making it enjoyable for much of the year. However, for the most pleasant experience, consider cycling during spring or early autumn when the weather is typically milder and the Galician countryside is at its most vibrant.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, it is generally suitable for cyclists with dogs in appropriate carriers or trailers. However, always ensure your dog is safe and comfortable, and adhere to local regulations regarding pets, especially when visiting historical sites or natural areas.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route. The trail passes through ZEC Brañas de Xestoso, a Special Area of Conservation. While no fees apply, visitors are expected to respect the natural environment and any local regulations regarding protected areas.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Senda Peonil da Ponte do Ramo (10% of the route), Caminho da Geira e dos Arrieiros (8%), and Sendeiro Rural de Galicia (7%). Smaller portions also cross the Mtb Vía Mariana (2%) and Via Mariana (2%).

faqs.question: What should I bring for a road bike tour in Cuntis?
faqs.answer: Given the 'moderate' difficulty and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring a sturdy road bike, appropriate cycling gear, and plenty of water. Due to potential unpaved sections, consider tires that can handle mixed surfaces. Always carry a repair kit, navigation tools, and layers of clothing for changing weather.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route or near the highlights?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through rural landscapes, so dedicated cafes directly on the trail might be sparse. However, you are likely to find options in Cuntis Historic Center at the start/end of your tour. Near the Church of San Jorge de Codeseda, local establishments might be available in the nearby village of Codeseda, though it's always good to carry some snacks and drinks.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and the presence of 'relentless hills' and significant elevation changes, this route might be challenging for absolute beginner cyclists. It is better suited for those with good fitness and some experience with road cycling on varied terrain.
